sprint: Companion Platform v1 (Sprint C1, Lumo v1)
task: Extends the prior sprint's Companion Engine Foundation into the full, frozen Companion Platform the follow-up spec asked for: a 3-file Companion Package Contract (companion.json + optional personality.json [traits/role/greetings/neverSays] + optional animations.json [state-transition/duration table]), a companion registry (assets/companions/registry.json), and a frozen CompanionEngine public API -- load/unload/show/hide/setState/getState/speak/wake/sleep/destroy. Timing that belongs to the companion itself now lives entirely in the loaded package's own animations.json instead of hardcoded Director-side setTimeout constants: setState(state) auto-schedules a transition to animations.transitions[state] after animations.durations[state] ms (Lumo's own wave:3000/celebrate:2000, matching the spec exactly), cancelled/rescheduled by any later setState() call; wake()/sleep() are semantic convenience wrappers (wake() -> setState('wave'), which auto-settles to idle per that same package data); speak()'s own bubble-dismiss timer settles a 'talk' state to animations.transitions.talk when the bubble auto-hides. js/companionDirector.js now has zero hardcoded companion-animation-timing constants (verified via a static scan) -- only Studio's own genuinely separate policy (2-minute idle-to-sleep, 4s typing cooldown) remains there. The boot greeting now prefers one of Lumo's own personality.json greetings (picked at random each boot) over a hardcoded default. The default companion to boot is read from registry.json's first entry, with 'lumo' kept as the one, final, disclosed literal fallback (still exactly one quoted 'lumo' in companionDirector.js, statically verified) -- adding a second companion (Nimbus, Moss, Nova) is now purely a new asset folder plus one registry.json entry, proven directly with two hand-authored throwaway test packages exercising a different state vocabulary and the optional 'blink' state, never referenced anywhere in the engine/director source. Companion UI gained genuine 'float above Studio UI, never block controls, draggable with per-session-remembered position, automatically avoid overlapping dialogs or menus' behaviour: the widget is pointer-events:none except its portrait circle (the one draggable surface), dragging switches it to explicit clamped left/top pixel positioning persisted to sessionStorage and restored on a same-tab reload; js/companionDirector.js -- the one file allowed to know Studio's own dialog system -- runs a MutationObserver watching a small, disclosed set of known Studio overlay containers (restore-session modal, theme-picker modal, Publish Studio modal, Magic Card overlay) and hides the companion outright whenever any is open, restoring it afterward only if it was visible before, satisfying 'avoid overlapping dialogs' without needing to know any future dialog's exact geometry. Micro polish (all CSS/Canvas, no sprite animation): show()/hide() now fade via a real opacity/transform/delayed-visibility CSS transition instead of instant display:none; a continuous subtle breathing keyframe on the portrait (paused while dragging); a distinct hover micro-interaction; a soft scale pulse while 'talk' and a bounce while 'celebrate', both driven purely by a new data-companion-state attribute the engine sets with zero knowledge of what either name means; a floating 'Zzz' indicator (a real sibling DOM element, not a pseudo-element inside the portrait's own overflow:hidden clip, so it can float outside the circle) shown only while asleep; and a future-ready, currently-inert random blink loop that activates automatically only for a package that declares a 'blink' state in companion.json (Lumo declares none today, proven inert via the test suite). Verified via a new 46-assertion Playwright suite (package-contract validation for all 3 files, static zero-hardcoded-reference/zero-hardcoded-timing checks, the full live boot/think/curious/artwork/publish choreography, real mouse-driven drag + sessionStorage persistence + same-tab-reload restore, sleep-after-2-minutes/wake-to-wave-then-idle via Playwright's fake clock now correctly timed off the package's own 3000ms wave duration, the full unload/wake/sleep/destroy/reload-reuses-DOM API surface, the registry loader, the blink-state proof plus Lumo's-own-inertness proof, and the dialog-occlusion hide/restore proof) plus the full existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright regression suite (12 scripts) and both goldenBuild.js suites -- all passing unchanged, zero console errors introduced by this sprint (the two pre-existing, disclosed Supabase-CDN-network-blocked-sandbox failures are unrelated, confirmed via direct request-failure inspection). No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change; confined to the two existing companion modules, one new registry.json, two new Lumo data files, and CSS.
